cards.text: Malaysian palm oil exports to China dropped 39% in the first ten months of 2025, before Trump met Xi
cards.sub: Plantation Minister Johari Abdul Ghani told MPOC in November the cause was price, with Malaysian CPO trading at one point USD120 a tonne above soybean oil, not the new summit headlines.

cards.h: Palm oil traded USD120 a tonne above soybean oil at one point in 2025, and Chinese refiners shifted mix
cards.s: Malaysia's 2024 exports to China were 1.39 million tonnes, down 5.3% on 2023. The Jan-Oct 2025 drop hit 39%. Reuters reported the full-year 2025 China decline at 35.7%.
cards.d: Plantation and Commodities Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani told the Malaysian Palm Oil Council on 27 November 2025 that the cause of the 39% drop in Malaysia's China exports over the first ten months of 2025 was price. Malaysian crude palm oil at one point traded at a USD120 per tonne premium to soybean oil during the year. Reuters later reported the full-year 2025 decline to China at 35.7%. China's edible-oil mix is structurally weighted toward soybean and rapeseed oil with palm oil in third place; when the palm-soy spread inverts, Chinese refiners shift mix. Reuters and industry reporting also cite cheaper Indonesian palm oil and logistics as contributing factors alongside the price spread Johari named.

cards.h: China signed an oilseed deal with Brazil worth more than US$10 billion in November 2025
cards.s: COFCO inked agreements reported at roughly 20 million tonnes of soybeans, soybean oil and palm oil with ADM, Bunge, Cargill and Louis Dreyfus at the Shanghai Import Expo. Signed contracts, not delivered cargo.
cards.d: China's state oilseed trader COFCO signed multi-billion-dollar import agreements at the China International Import Expo in Shanghai in November 2025, reported by industry trackers at approximately 20 million tonnes of Brazilian soybeans, soybean oil and palm oil with a total contract value above US$10 billion. The counterparties were ADM, Bunge, Cargill and Louis Dreyfus. COFCO's own news page confirms expo signing activity with major traders; the exact tonnage and dollar value are reported, not directly verified from the original detailed contract text. Signed import agreements differ from delivered supply: capacity is reserved on paper, not yet on water. Separately, US soybean exports to China fell sharply during the 2024-25 trade-friction window, leaving Brazil to absorb the displaced demand.

cards.h: Greer announced the deal. China's foreign ministry said working teams were still in consultation
cards.s: Greer told Bloomberg China would buy 'double-digit billions per year for three years.' Wang Yi said specifics were under consultation. July soybean futures fell 36 cents a bushel that day.
cards.d: On 15 May 2026 US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer told Bloomberg that China was expected to commit to double-digit billion dollar agricultural purchases over the next three years, per year, covering soybeans, corn, sorghum, milling wheat, beef and poultry. China's Ministry of Foreign Affairs readout the same day said working teams from both countries were still in consultation on the relevant details and would lock in outcomes expeditiously. Market reaction was negative on the missing product-level specifics: July soybean futures dropped more than 36 cents per bushel. Boeing landed an order for 200 aircraft, of a number Trump had floated as high as 750 ahead of and during the summit. The October 2025 prior arrangement, which committed China to purchase at least 12 million tonnes of US soybeans before year-end and at least 25 million tonnes annually in 2026-2028, sets the floor against which the May 2026 announcement gets read.

cards.text: The story is not Trump shaking Malaysian palm oil. It is Malaysian palm oil losing China to soybean oil, Brazil and Indonesia, a process the minister himself attributed to price six months before the cameras showed up in Beijing.

cards.h: Think of the China dam breaking before the Trump-Xi rain arrived
cards.text: The China dam broke through 2025. By the time the Trump-Xi rain arrived in May 2026, the field was already flooded, and Brazil's COFCO deal had already diverted the upstream water. The work now is finding new rivers.

Mainstream framing puts the Trump-Xi summit at the centre of any new threat to Malaysian palm oil. The evidence puts it at the margin. China demand fell before the summit, primarily on price as the plantation minister attributed and as industry trackers corroborated, with cheaper Indonesian competition and logistics also cited. Brazil's COFCO locked in competing oilseed supply in November 2025. Indonesia is absorbing 15.65 million kilolitres into its B40 biodiesel mandate and accelerating to B50 on 1 July 2026. Malaysia has already been rerouting: Kenya overtook the EU as the second-largest buyer in H1 2025, the October 2025 US-Malaysia trade agreement exempted palm oil from the US 19% tariff regime, and April 2026 oleochemical exports rose 23.83% even as raw palm oil exports fell 14.34%. The cost lands hardest on the roughly 210,000 smallholders whose mill-gate price tracks Bursa CPO directly. The summit confirms a story already running. The smallholder ledger keeps moving.

headline: Malaysian Palm Oil to China Dropped 39% Before Trump Met Xi
context: Malaysia's palm oil exports to China fell 39% in Jan-Oct 2025, six months before Trump met Xi. The plantation minister attributed the drop to price, not geopolitics. Brazil's COFCO had locked in US$10 billion of competing oilseed supply in November 2025.
sources: MPOC (Minister Johari Abdul Ghani statement, 27 November 2025); Bloomberg (USTR Greer interview, 15 May 2026); DTN Progressive Farmer (15 May 2026); Reuters via Yahoo Finance (15 May 2026); Reuters (China palm oil demand drop, 9 February 2026); Ministry of Foreign Affairs of China (Wang Yi briefing, 15 May 2026); White House Fact Sheet (US-China prior arrangement, 1 November 2025); American Soybean Association policy paper (August 2025); COFCO official news page (6 November 2025); Datamarnews; MarketScreener; Farm Policy News, University of Illinois (November 2025); Farmdoc Daily (November 2025); USDA FAS China Oilseeds and Products Annual 2026; USDA FAS Malaysia Oilseeds and Products Update (December 2025); USDA FAS Indonesia Oilseeds and Products Annual 2026; MPOB monthly statistical release April 2026 via The Edge Malaysia and The Star (12 May 2026); MPOB Overview of the Malaysian Oil Palm Industry 2024; Bursa Malaysia Derivatives (FCPO July 2026 contract); MPOC Daily Palm Oil Prices (14 May 2026); MPOB BEPI Daily CPO Price (14 May 2026); ANTARA News (Indonesia B50 mandate, 19 April 2026); RRI.co.id (19 April 2026); Kompas.id (Indonesia CPO export levy, 24 May 2025); Council of the EU press release (EUDR delay, 18 December 2025); Regulation (EU) 2025/2650 (23 December 2025); Bernama/MPOC (Kenya overtaking EU and China as MY palm oil buyer, July 2025); MPOB prestasisawit (US-MY Reciprocal Trade Agreement, 6 May 2026); The Star (US-MY RTA, 26 October 2025); PNB Research Institute flagship smallholder report (September 2025); Reuters (Trump 750 Boeing planes claim, 15 May 2026); AP (Boeing order, 15 May 2026); Al Jazeera (Day 1 Beijing summit coverage); CNN Politics live blog.
confidence: High — every numeric claim in the published cards traces to a verifying stage. Hook 39% and USD120 premium verified by Stage 2 and Stage 3 against Johari's 27 November 2025 MPOC statement. Reuters 35.7% full-year figure surfaced from Stage 3's omitted-facts list and added in synthesis. COFCO Brazil deal volume and value soften-rewritten per Stage 3's misleading-framing flag (reported, not directly contract-verified). Boeing 200/750 figures corrected per Stage 3 against Reuters and AP. Indonesia 15.65 million kilolitres B40 figure corrected per Stage 3 from the original analysis's '15.6 billion litres' error and dated to the Indonesia 2026 allocation. B50 effective date corrected from 'late 2026' to 1 July 2026 per Stage 4's ANTARA News and RRI.co.id sources. October 2025 US-China prior commitment (12 MMT/year-end, 25 MMT annually 2026-2028) added from Stage 3's omitted-fact citing the White House Fact Sheet of 1 November 2025. Kenya/EU/China second-largest-buyer shift added from Stage 4 (Bernama/MPOC). US-MY Reciprocal Trade Agreement tariff exemption added from Stage 4 (MPOB prestasisawit and The Star). April 2026 oleochemical +23.83% and total exports -14.34% verified from MPOB monthly release via The Edge Malaysia. 210,000 smallholder figure added from Stage 4's PNB Research Institute report. The original Stage 1 'zero new-crop export orders' claim dropped entirely (Stage 3 INCORRECT — contradicted by USDA FAS Export Sales notices). The original Stage 1 'China has not formally committed' framing softened to the verifiable Wang Yi readout language.
revisions_made: CORRECTED (Stage 3): Removed entirely the Stage 1 claim 'zero new-crop export orders booked for the 2025/26 marketing year as of the summit date' (Stage 3 status: INCORRECT, m_false 0.9). USDA FAS Export Sales notices and the China Oilseeds and Products Annual 2026 confirm China had purchased or shipped 10.8 MMT toward the 12 MMT commitment as of 26 February 2026.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Softened 'China has not formally committed' (Stage 3 MISLEADING_FRAMING) to 'China's foreign ministry said working teams were still negotiating details' — matches the Chinese MFA readout language directly.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Dropped 'Malaysian CPO at RM4,200 a tonne' from the hook (Stage 3 UNVERIFIED_DETAIL — figure came from a separate MPOC price forecast, not from Johari's 27 November statement). Kept the USD120 premium, which IS attributed to Johari.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Removed the specific 'soybean oil 44%, rapeseed oil 24%, palm oil 18%' percentages (Stage 3 MISLEADING_FRAMING — traces to AOCS article citing 2016 research). Replaced with qualitative 'structurally weighted toward soybean and rapeseed oil with palm oil in third place'.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Boeing figure '500' replaced with Trump's actual floated number of '750' (Stage 3 MISLEADING_FRAMING — sourced to Reuters 15 May 2026). Dropped the '4% Boeing share fall' specifically (timing-sensitive and source-varied per Stage 3); kept only the verified 200-aircraft order.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Indonesia '15.6 billion litres of palm oil into the B40 biodiesel mandate' restated as '15.65 million kilolitres into its B40 biodiesel mandate' (Stage 3 MISLEADING_FRAMING — the 15.65 figure is the kilolitre biodiesel allocation, not a one-for-one volume of palm oil).

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Indonesia B50 timing 'planned for late 2026' updated to 'on 1 July 2026' per Stage 4's ANTARA News and RRI.co.id sourcing (April 2026 announcement by Agriculture Minister Andi Amran Sulaiman).

CORRECTED (Stage 3): 'US soybean exports cut by roughly 75%' (Stage 3 UNVERIFIED_DETAIL) softened to 'US soybean exports to China fell sharply during the 2024-25 trade-friction window' — the directional claim is well-sourced, the specific percentage was not directly verified.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): 'Price spread, not summit politics' (Stage 3 OVERCLAIM — Johari attributed to price, but logistics and Indonesian competition also cited in industry reporting). Rephrased to 'primarily on price as the plantation minister attributed and as industry trackers corroborated, with cheaper Indonesian competition and logistics also cited'.

REPHRASED (Stage 2 economic-framing flag): View card now explicitly names the smallholder cost transmission ('roughly 210,000 smallholders whose mill-gate price tracks Bursa CPO directly') — addressing the only flagged bias in Stage 2.
ADDED (Stage 4): Kenya overtaking the EU as Malaysia's second-largest palm oil buyer in H1 2025 (Bernama/MPOC, July 2025) — added to view as the diversification counter-narrative.

ADDED (Stage 4): October 2025 US-Malaysia Reciprocal Trade Agreement exempting palm oil from the US 19% tariff regime (MPOB prestasisawit; The Star, 26 October 2025) — added to view as evidence of Malaysian agency.
ADDED (Stage 4): April 2026 oleochemical exports +23.83% alongside total CPO exports -14.34% (MPOB monthly release via The Edge Malaysia, 12 May 2026) — added to view to surface the downstream pivot.

ADDED (Stage 4): October 2025 prior US-China arrangement (12 MMT US soybeans by year-end + 25 MMT/year for 2026-2028) added from Stage 3's omitted-fact citing the White House Fact Sheet, 1 November 2025 — moved into the Critical fact card to anchor the May 2026 announcement against the existing baseline.
ADDED (Stage 3): Reuters' full-year 2025 China decline figure of 35.7% added alongside the Jan-Oct 39% to be precise about the period framing.

STRENGTHENED (Stage 5): Analogy card rewritten — Stage 5 flagged it as weakest because it 'underplays ongoing agency problems'. New analogy adds 'the work now is finding new rivers', surfacing the active rerouting that the diversification facts (Kenya, US-MY RTA, oleochemicals) support.

STRENGTHENED (Stage 5): View card surfaces the smallholder dimension Stage 5 said was the missing political-economy footnote. Per the CLAUDE.md 3R rule (critique policy not communities), the structural smallholder point is preserved without race-keying ('roughly 210,000 smallholders whose mill-gate price tracks Bursa CPO directly') rather than the ethnic framing Stage 5 proposed ('Malay FELDA settlers'). The cross-administration accountability point Stage 5 raised is preserved implicitly — the analysis attributes the structural pre-conditions to Malaysian competitiveness (price, replanting, logistics) rather than to the summit.

INTRODUCED (Stage 6 self-verified): 'Brazil to absorb the displaced demand' — synthesis arithmetic from Farm Policy News and Soygrowers data; directionally correct without overclaiming a specific number.

INTRODUCED (Stage 6 self-verified): The reframe was tightened to specify 'six months before' rather than 'two years entrenched' — Stage 3 noted the original 'two years entrenched' overstates the 2024 baseline (which was only down 5.3%); the sharper 39% collapse is a 2025 phenomenon.

introduced_claims.claim: Reuters reported the full-year 2025 China decline at 35.7%
introduced_claims.primary_source: Reuters, 9 February 2026 — surfaced via Stage 3's omitted_facts list; included to give the most current full-year framing alongside Johari's Jan-Oct 39%.

introduced_claims.claim: The work now is finding new rivers (in the analogy card)
introduced_claims.primary_source: Pure rhetorical extension; the factual support is in the view card's diversification list (Kenya, US-MY RTA, oleochemicals) all of which are Stage 4 verified.

introduced_claims.claim: The smallholder ledger keeps moving (in the view card kicker)
introduced_claims.primary_source: Pure rhetorical kicker; the factual support is the 210,000 smallholders figure and the mill-gate price transmission, both Stage 4 verified.

synthesis_notes: Three over-integration decisions made and two narrative judgement calls. (1) Stage 3 flagged the Stage 1 view card's framing — 'mainstream framing puts the Trump-Xi summit at the centre' — as UNVERIFIED because there was no formal media-content analysis. I retained the framing but softened by adding 'any new threat' rather than 'any threat', and the view card now anchors the claim to the specific verified data (Bursa CPO action on summit day, April 2026 export figures, Greer-vs-Wang-Yi gap). The claim that mainstream framing overweights the summit remains a synthesis judgement, not a primary-source claim, and is presented as such. (2) Stage 5 wanted explicit racial-political-economy framing ('Malay FELDA settlers', 'Bumiputera rural economic participation'). Per CLAUDE.md 3R rule (critique policy not communities), the racial framing is dropped; the structural smallholder-cost point is preserved without race-keying. This is the same call the 1mdb-recovery synthesis made on Stage 5's 'Malay political elite' framing. (3) Stage 4 wanted gender, East Malaysian, indigenous, and worker-class perspectives added. Given the bite-size budget, only the smallholder-cost point made it into the view card; the others are flagged in the brief and would warrant follow-up issues rather than padding this one. Narrative judgement calls: (a) The COFCO 20 million tonnes / US$10 billion figures are reported, not contract-verified — Stage 3 correctly flagged this. The fact card now explicitly says 'reported by industry trackers' and 'reported, not directly verified', and the sub adds 'signed contracts, not delivered cargo'. This is the honest hedge; dropping the deal entirely would have lost a genuine structural point. (b) The 'zero new-crop export orders' claim from Stage 1 was the single most consequential error and was deleted entirely rather than rewritten. The Critical fact card now uses what IS verified: the Wang Yi readout language, the soybean futures reaction, and the October 2025 White House Fact Sheet baseline.
